caption: Cycle charge against a target that breaks and flees: the first impact routs the infantry, and the cavalry now retires at a trot, brakes onto the 280 wu standoff instead of galloping through the turn-around with full momentum, wheels about, and canters back in to drive its second charge home around t950-1000. (The broken foe rallies while the horse is out at the standoff -- with no enemy nearby to keep it running -- so the second charge slams into a re-formed line; the re-engagement is the point.) Previously the pursuer receded away from the router indefinitely: the peel galloped past the standoff, the soldier bodies dragged the regiment further out, and the walk-pace re-approach could never close the distance again.
